banner: DRAFT — NOT SUBMITTED — review with a human before proceeding
scenario: Illustrative mock company: 'Tiong Bahru Provisions Pte. Ltd.', a Singapore-incorporated general wholesale trading company with a 31 Dec financial year-end, filing Form C-S for Year of Assessment (YA) 2026 (basis period 1 Jan 2025 to 31 Dec 2025). Revenue for the period is S$850,000 (within the S$200,000-S$5,000,000 Form C-S band). The Part B tax-adjustment figures reproduce, field-by-field, the worked 'Computation of Tax Payable Annex' example in IRAS' own Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026 (net profit $150,000 through to tax payable $7,898.15 after the New Start-up Companies exemption and CIT Rebate), to confirm this schema's fields can carry that official worked example without alteration. Part C financial-statement figures (revenue, gross profit, remuneration, etc.) are fabricated illustrative amounts, not sourced from the Annex. All names, numbers, and identifiers are fabricated test data, not a real company's information.

collectedValues.label: Item 36: New Start-up Companies tax exemption qualifying conditions met
collectedValues.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes Annex — the worked example's tax-payable figure uses the automatic Partial Tax Exemption available to all companies (reference 'v' on the 'First S$10,000 @75%'/'Next S$133,780 @50%' lines), not the New Start-up Companies scheme; the Annex's separate page-13 New Start-up Companies calculation ('Total Exempt Amount 96,890') is presented only as an alternative 'if applicable' illustration and is not the calculation that produces the published S$7,898.15 tax-payable figure — this scenario's fictional company does not claim it

computedForReference.note: The following are NOT schema fields (myTax Portal computes them automatically per the User Guide's own list) but are reproduced here to show the worked example carries through correctly using only this schema's fields as inputs.

adjustedProfitBeforeCapitalAllowances.formula: netProfitOrLossBeforeTax(150000) - separateSourceIncome(42900) - nonTaxableIncome(1000) + nonTaxDeductibleExpenses(7630) - renovationRefurbishmentDeductionS14N(0) - enterpriseInnovationSchemeDeductions(0) - item8FurtherOrOtherDeductions(0)

partialTaxExemptAmount.formula: automatic Partial Tax Exemption available to all companies (not claimsStartupTaxExemption, which is false in this scenario): 75% of first 10,000 (7,500) + 50% of next 133,780 (66,890)

citRebateNet.formula: 50% of 11,796.30 (5,898.15) less CIT Rebate Cash Grant of 2,000

taxPayable.sourceRef: Explanatory Notes to Form C-S for YA 2026, 'Computation of Tax Payable Annex'

conformanceCheck.method: Programmatic re-derivation of every field's effective requiredness from the schema's own requiredWhen/required members (a GSP-0013 condition evaluator), cross-checked against collectedValues + notApplicableFields for exact 1:1 coverage of all 55 fields in schema.fields. Mutation tests confirmed the returnReviewedByAccreditedTaxProfessional, substantialChangeInShareholders, and foreignAssetDisposalGains10L requiredWhen gates all flip correctly, and that the Part B/Part C amounts reproduce the Explanatory Notes' own worked tax-computation example through to the published tax-payable figure of S$7,898.15 using only this schema's field set as inputs (see computedForReference).
